NKJV.1: Then He began to speak to them in parables: “A man planted a vineyard and set a hedge around it, dug a place for the wine vat and built a tower. And he leased it to vinedressers and went into a far country.
NKJV.2: Now at vintage-time he sent a servant to the vinedressers, that he might receive some of the fruit of the vineyard from the vinedressers.
NKJV.3: And they took him and beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
NKJV.4: Again he sent them another servant, and at him they threw stones, wounded him in the head, and sent him away shamefully treated.
NKJV.5: And again he sent another, and him they killed; and many others,  beating some and killing some.
NKJV.6: Therefore still having one son, his beloved, he also sent him to them last, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
NKJV.7: But those vinedressers said among themselves, ‘This is the heir. Come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’
NKJV.8: So they took him and killed him and cast him out of the vineyard.
NKJV.9: “Therefore what will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the vinedressers, and give the vineyard to others.
NKJV.10: Have you not even read this Scripture:  ‘The stone which the builders rejected Has become the chief cornerstone.
NKJV.11: This was the LORD’s doing, And it is marvelous in our eyes’?”
NKJV.12: And they sought to lay hands on Him, but feared the multitude, for they knew He had spoken the parable against them. So they left Him and went away.
NKJV.13: Then they sent to Him some of the Pharisees and the Herodians, to catch Him in His words.
NKJV.14: When they had come, they said to Him, “Teacher, we know that You are true, and care about no one; for You do not regard the person of men, but teach the way of God in truth. Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?
NKJV.15: Shall we pay, or shall we not pay?” But He, knowing their hypocrisy, said to them, “Why do you test Me? Bring Me a denarius that I may see it.”
NKJV.16: So they brought it. And He said to them, “Whose image and inscription is this?” They said to Him, “Caesar’s.”
NKJV.17: And Jesus answered and said to them, “Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to  God the things that are God’s.” And they marveled at Him.
NKJV.18: Then some Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to Him; and they asked Him, saying:
NKJV.19: “Teacher, Moses wrote to us that if a man’s brother dies, and leaves his wife behind, and leaves no children, his brother should take his wife and raise up offspring for his brother.
NKJV.20: Now there were seven brothers. The first took a wife; and dying, he left no offspring.
NKJV.21: And the second took her, and he died; nor did he leave any offspring. And the third likewise.
NKJV.22: So the seven had her and left no offspring. Last of all the woman died also.
NKJV.23: Therefore, in the resurrection, when they rise, whose wife will she be? For all seven had her as wife.”
NKJV.24: Jesus answered and said to them, “Are you not therefore mistaken, because you do not know the Scriptures nor the power of God?
NKJV.25: For when they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but  are like angels in heaven.
NKJV.26: But concerning the dead, that they rise, have you not read in the book of Moses, in the burning bush passage, how God spoke to him, saying, ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’?
NKJV.27: He is not the God of the dead, but the God of the living. You are therefore greatly mistaken.”

NKJV.29: Jesus answered him, “The first of all the commandments is: ‘Hear, O Israel, the LORD our God, the LORD is one.
NKJV.30: And you shall love the LORD your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your mind, and with all your strength.’ This is the first commandment.
NKJV.31: And the second, like it, is this: ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’ There is no other commandment greater than these.”

NKJV.37: Therefore David himself calls Him ‘Lord’; how is He then his Son?” And the common people heard Him gladly.
NKJV.38: Then He said to them in His teaching, “Beware of the scribes, who desire to go around in long robes, love greetings in the marketplaces,
NKJV.39: the  best seats in the synagogues, and the best places at feasts,
NKJV.40: who devour widows’ houses, and for a pretense make long prayers. These will receive greater condemnation.”

NKJV.44: for they all put in out of their abundance, but she out of her poverty put in all that she had,  her whole livelihood.”

ESV.1: And he began to speak to them in parables. “A man planted a vineyard and put a fence around it and dug a pit for the winepress and built a tower, and leased it to tenants and went into another country.
ESV.2: When the season came, he sent a servant  to the tenants to get from them some of the fruit of the vineyard.
ESV.3: And they took him and beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
ESV.4: Again he sent to them another servant, and they struck him on the head and treated him shamefully.
ESV.5: And he sent another, and him they killed. And so with many others: some they beat, and some they killed.
ESV.6: He had still one other, a beloved son. Finally he sent him to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
ESV.7: But those tenants said to one another, ‘This is the heir. Come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’
ESV.8: And they took him and killed him and threw him out of the vineyard.
ESV.9: What will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the tenants and give the vineyard to others.
ESV.10: Have you not read this Scripture:  “‘The stone that the builders rejected has become the cornerstone;
ESV.11: this was the Lord's doing, and it is marvelous in our eyes’?”
ESV.12: And they were seeking to arrest him but feared the people, for they perceived that he had told the parable against them. So they left him and went away.
ESV.13: And they sent to him some of the Pharisees and some of the Herodians, to trap him in his talk.
ESV.14: And they came and said to him, “Teacher, we know that you are true and do not care about anyone's opinion. For you are not swayed by appearances,  but truly teach the way of God. Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not? Should we pay them, or should we not?”
ESV.15: But, knowing their hypocrisy, he said to them, “Why put me to the test? Bring me a denarius  and let me look at it.”
ESV.16: And they brought one. And he said to them, “Whose likeness and inscription is this?” They said to him, “Caesar's.”
ESV.17: Jesus said to them,  “Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's.” And they marveled at him.
ESV.18: And Sadducees came to him, who say that there is no resurrection. And they asked him a question, saying,
ESV.19: “Teacher, Moses wrote for us that if a man's brother dies and leaves a wife, but leaves no child, the man  must take the widow and raise up offspring for his brother.
ESV.20: There were seven brothers; the first took a wife, and when he died left no offspring.
ESV.21: And the second took her, and died, leaving no offspring. And the third likewise.
ESV.22: And the seven left no offspring. Last of all the woman also died.
ESV.23: In the resurrection, when they rise again, whose wife will she be? For the seven had her as wife.”
ESV.24: Jesus said to them, “Is this not the reason you are wrong, because you know neither the Scriptures nor the power of God?
ESV.25: For when they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
ESV.26: And as for the dead being raised, have you not read in the book of Moses, in the passage about the bush, how God spoke to him, saying, ‘I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’?
ESV.27: He is not God of the dead, but of the living. You are quite wrong.”
ESV.28: And one of the scribes came up and heard them disputing with one another, and seeing that he answered them well, asked him, “Which commandment is the most important of all?”
ESV.29: Jesus answered, “The most important is, ‘Hear, O Israel: The Lord our God, the Lord is one.
ESV.30: And you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind and with all your strength.’
ESV.31: The second is this: ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’ There is no other commandment greater than these.”
ESV.32: And the scribe said to him, “You are right, Teacher. You have truly said that he is one, and there is no other besides him.
ESV.33: And to love him with all the heart and with all the understanding and with all the strength, and to love one's neighbor as oneself, is much more than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.”
ESV.34: And when Jesus saw that he answered wisely, he said to him, “You are not far from the kingdom of God.” And after that no one dared to ask him any more questions.
ESV.35: And as Jesus taught in the temple, he said, “How can the scribes say that the Christ is the son of David?
ESV.36: David himself, in the Holy Spirit, declared,  “‘The Lord said to my Lord, “Sit at my right hand, until I put your enemies under your feet.”’
ESV.37: David himself calls him Lord. So how is he his son?” And the great throng heard him gladly.
ESV.38: And in his teaching he said, “Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes and like greetings in the marketplaces
ESV.39: and have the best seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at feasts,
ESV.40: who devour widows' houses and for a pretense make long prayers. They will receive the greater condemnation.”
ESV.41: And he sat down opposite the treasury and watched the people putting money into the offering box. Many rich people put in large sums.
ESV.42: And a poor widow came and put in two small copper coins, which make a penny.
ESV.43: And he called his disciples to him and said to them, “Truly, I say to you, this poor widow has put in more than all those who are contributing to the offering box.
ESV.44: For they all contributed out of their abundance, but she out of her poverty has put in everything she had, all she had to live on.”

NASB.2: 'At the [harvest] time he sent a slave to the vine-growers, in order to receive [some] of the produce of the vineyard from the vine-growers.

NASB.4: 'Again he sent them another slave, and they wounded him in the head, and treated him shamefully.
NASB.5: 'And he sent another, and that one they killed; and [so with] many others, beating some and killing others.
NASB.6: 'He had one more [to send], a beloved son; he sent him last [of all] to them, saying, 'They will respect my son.'
NASB.7: 'But those vine-growers said to one another, 'This is the heir; come, let us kill him, and the inheritance will be ours!'
NASB.8: 'They took him, and killed him and threw him out of the vineyard.

NASB.19: 'Teacher, Moses wrote for us that IF A MAN'S BROTHER DIES and leaves behind a wife AND LEAVES NO CHILD, HIS BROTHER SHOULD MARRY THE WIFE AND RAISE UP CHILDREN TO HIS BROTHER.
NASB.20: 'There were seven brothers; and the first took a wife, and died leaving no children.
NASB.21: 'The second one married her, and died leaving behind no children; and the third likewise;
NASB.22: and [so] all seven left no children. Last of all the woman died also.
NASB.23: 'In the resurrection, when they rise again, which one's wife will she be? For all seven had married her.'
NASB.24: Jesus said to them, 'Is this not the reason you are mistaken, that you do not understand the Scriptures or the power of God?
NASB.25: 'For when they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
NASB.26: 'But regarding the fact that the dead rise again, have you not read in the book of Moses, in the [passage] about [the burning] bush, how God spoke to him, saying, 'I AM THE GOD OF ABRAHAM, AND THE GOD OF ISAAC, and the God of Jacob '?
NASB.27: 'He is not the God of the dead, but of the living; you are greatly mistaken.'
NASB.28: One of the scribes came and heard them arguing, and recognizing that He had answered them well, asked Him, 'What commandment is the foremost of all?'
NASB.29: Jesus answered, 'The foremost is, 'HEAR, O ISRAEL! THE LORD OUR GOD IS ONE LORD;
NASB.30: AND YOU SHALL LOVE THE LORD YOUR GOD WITH ALL YOUR HEART, AND WITH ALL YOUR SOUL, AND WITH ALL YOUR MIND, AND WITH ALL YOUR STRENGTH.'
NASB.31: 'The second is this, 'YOU SHALL LOVE YOUR NEIGHBOR AS YOURSELF.' There is no other commandment greater than these.'
NASB.32: The scribe said to Him, 'Right, Teacher; You have truly stated that HE IS ONE, AND THERE IS NO ONE ELSE BESIDES HIM;

NASB.34: When Jesus saw that he had answered intelligently, He said to him, 'You are not far from the kingdom of God.' After that, no one would venture to ask Him any more questions.

NLT.1: Then Jesus began teaching them with stories: “A man planted a vineyard. He built a wall around it, dug a pit for pressing out the grape juice, and built a lookout tower. Then he leased the vineyard to tenant farmers and moved to another country.
NLT.2: At the time of the grape harvest, he sent one of his servants to collect his share of the crop.
NLT.3: But the farmers grabbed the servant, beat him up, and sent him back empty-handed.
NLT.4: The owner then sent another servant, but they insulted him and beat him over the head.
NLT.5: The next servant he sent was killed. Others he sent were either beaten or killed,
NLT.6: until there was only one left — his son whom he loved dearly. The owner finally sent him, thinking, ‘Surely they will respect my son.’
NLT.7: “But the tenant farmers said to one another, ‘Here comes the heir to this estate. Let’s kill him and get the estate for ourselves!’
NLT.8: So they grabbed him and murdered him and threw his body out of the vineyard.
NLT.9: “What do you suppose the owner of the vineyard will do?” Jesus asked. “I’ll tell you — he will come and kill those farmers and lease the vineyard to others.
NLT.10:
Didn’t you ever read this in the Scriptures? ‘The stone that the builders rejected
has now become the cornerstone.
NLT.11:
This is the LORD’s doing,
and it is wonderful to see.’ ”
NLT.12: The religious leaders wanted to arrest Jesus because they realized he was telling the story against them — they were the wicked farmers. But they were afraid of the crowd, so they left him and went away.
NLT.13: Later the leaders sent some Pharisees and supporters of Herod to trap Jesus into saying something for which he could be arrested.
NLT.14: “Teacher,” they said, “we know how honest you are. You are impartial and don’t play favorites. You teach the way of God truthfully. Now tell us — is it right to pay taxes to Caesar or not?
NLT.15: Should we pay them, or shouldn’t we?” Jesus saw through their hypocrisy and said, “Why are you trying to trap me? Show me a Roman coin, and I’ll tell you.”
NLT.16: When they handed it to him, he asked, “Whose picture and title are stamped on it?” “Caesar’s,” they replied.
NLT.17: “Well, then,” Jesus said, “give to Caesar what belongs to Caesar, and give to God what belongs to God.” His reply completely amazed them.
NLT.18: Then Jesus was approached by some Sadducees — religious leaders who say there is no resurrection from the dead. They posed this question:
NLT.19: “Teacher, Moses gave us a law that if a man dies, leaving a wife without children, his brother should marry the widow and have a child who will carry on the brother’s name.
NLT.20: Well, suppose there were seven brothers. The oldest one married and then died without children.
NLT.21: So the second brother married the widow, but he also died without children. Then the third brother married her.
NLT.22: This continued with all seven of them, and still there were no children. Last of all, the woman also died.
NLT.23: So tell us, whose wife will she be in the resurrection? For all seven were married to her.”
NLT.24: Jesus replied, “Your mistake is that you don’t know the Scriptures, and you don’t know the power of God.
NLT.25: For when the dead rise, they will neither marry nor be given in marriage. In this respect they will be like the angels in heaven.
NLT.26: “But now, as to whether the dead will be raised — haven’t you ever read about this in the writings of Moses, in the story of the burning bush? Long after Abraham, Isaac, and Jacob had died, God said to Moses, ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob.’
NLT.27: So he is the God of the living, not the dead. You have made a serious error.”
NLT.28: One of the teachers of religious law was standing there listening to the debate. He realized that Jesus had answered well, so he asked, “Of all the commandments, which is the most important?”
NLT.29: Jesus replied, “The most important commandment is this: ‘Listen, O Israel! The LORD our God is the one and only LORD.
NLT.30: And you must love the LORD your God with all your heart, all your soul, all your mind, and all your strength.’
NLT.31: The second is equally important: ‘Love your neighbor as yourself.’ No other commandment is greater than these.”
NLT.32: The teacher of religious law replied, “Well said, Teacher. You have spoken the truth by saying that there is only one God and no other.
NLT.33: And I know it is important to love him with all my heart and all my understanding and all my strength, and to love my neighbor as myself. This is more important than to offer all of the burnt offerings and sacrifices required in the law.”
NLT.34: Realizing how much the man understood, Jesus said to him, “You are not far from the Kingdom of God.” And after that, no one dared to ask him any more questions.
NLT.35: Later, as Jesus was teaching the people in the Temple, he asked, “Why do the teachers of religious law claim that the Messiah is the son of David?
NLT.36:
For David himself, speaking under the inspiration of the Holy Spirit, said, ‘The LORD said to my Lord,
Sit in the place of honor at my right hand
until I humble your enemies beneath your feet.’
NLT.37: Since David himself called the Messiah ‘my Lord,’ how can the Messiah be his son?” The large crowd listened to him with great delight.
NLT.38: Jesus also taught: “Beware of these teachers of religious law! For they like to parade around in flowing robes and receive respectful greetings as they walk in the marketplaces.
NLT.39: And how they love the seats of honor in the synagogues and the head table at banquets.
NLT.40: Yet they shamelessly cheat widows out of their property and then pretend to be pious by making long prayers in public. Because of this, they will be more severely punished.”
NLT.41: Jesus sat down near the collection box in the Temple and watched as the crowds dropped in their money. Many rich people put in large amounts.
NLT.42: Then a poor widow came and dropped in two small coins.
NLT.43: Jesus called his disciples to him and said, “I tell you the truth, this poor widow has given more than all the others who are making contributions.
NLT.44: For they gave a tiny part of their surplus, but she, poor as she is, has given everything she had to live on.”

WEB.1: He began to speak to them in parables. “A man planted a vineyard, put a hedge around it, dug a pit for the wine press, built a tower, rented it out to a farmer, and went into another country.
WEB.2: When it was time, he sent a servant to the farmer to get from the farmer his share of the fruit of the vineyard.
WEB.3: They took him, beat him, and sent him away empty.
WEB.4: Again, he sent another servant to them; and they threw stones at him, wounded him in the head, and sent him away shamefully treated.
WEB.5: Again he sent another; and they killed him; and many others, beating some, and killing some.
WEB.6: Therefore still having one, his beloved son, he sent him last to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
WEB.7: But those farmers said among themselves, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’
WEB.8: They took him, killed him, and cast him out of the vineyard.
WEB.9: What therefore will the lord of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the farmers, and will give the vineyard to others.
WEB.10: Haven’t you even read this Scripture:  ‘The stone which the builders rejected,  the same was made the head of the corner.
WEB.11: This was from the Lord,  it is marvelous in our eyes’?”
WEB.12: They tried to seize him, but they feared the multitude; for they perceived that he spoke the parable against them. They left him, and went away.
WEB.13: They sent some of the Pharisees and of the Herodians to him, that they might trap him with words.
WEB.14: When they had come, they asked him, “Teacher, we know that you are honest, and don’t defer to anyone; for you aren’t partial to anyone, but truly teach the way of God. Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar, or not?
WEB.15: Shall we give, or shall we not give?” But he, knowing their hypocrisy, said to them, “Why do you test me? Bring me a denarius, that I may see it.”
WEB.16: They brought it. He said to them, “Whose is this image and inscription?” They said to him, “Caesar’s.”
WEB.17: Jesus answered them, “Render to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.” They marveled greatly at him.
WEB.18: There came to him Sadducees, who say that there is no resurrection. They asked him, saying,
WEB.19: “Teacher, Moses wrote to us, ‘If a man’s brother dies, and leaves a wife behind him, and leaves no children, that his brother should take his wife, and raise up offspring for his brother.’
WEB.20: There were seven brothers. The first took a wife, and dying left no offspring.
WEB.21: The second took her, and died, leaving no children behind him. The third likewise;
WEB.22: and the seven took her and left no children. Last of all the woman also died.
WEB.23: In the resurrection, when they rise, whose wife will she be of them? For the seven had her as a wife.”
WEB.24: Jesus answered them, “Isn’t this because you are mistaken, not knowing the Scriptures, nor the power of God?
WEB.25: For when they will rise from the dead, they neither marry, nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
WEB.26: But about the dead, that they are raised; haven’t you read in the book of Moses, about the Bush, how God spoke to him, saying, ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’?
WEB.27: He is not the God of the dead, but of the living. You are therefore badly mistaken.”
WEB.28: One of the scribes came, and heard them questioning together. Knowing that he had answered them well, asked him, “Which commandment is the greatest of all?”
WEB.29: Jesus answered, “The greatest is, ‘Hear, Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is one:
WEB.30: you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul, and with all your mind, and with all your strength.’  This is the first commandment.
WEB.31: The second is like this, ‘You shall love your neighbor as yourself.’  There is no other commandment greater than these.”
WEB.32: The scribe said to him, “Truly, teacher, you have said well that he is one, and there is none other but he,
WEB.33: and to love him with all the heart, and with all the understanding, with all the soul, and with all the strength, and to love his neighbor as himself, is more important than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.”
WEB.34: When Jesus saw that he answered wisely, he said to him, “You are not far from God’s Kingdom.” No one dared ask him any question after that.
WEB.35: Jesus responded, as he taught in the temple, “How is it that the scribes say that the Christ is the son of David?
WEB.36: For David himself said in the Holy Spirit,  ‘The Lord said to my Lord,  “Sit at my right hand,  until I make your enemies the footstool of your feet.”’
WEB.37: Therefore David himself calls him Lord, so how can he be his son?”  The common people heard him gladly.
WEB.38: In his teaching he said to them, “Beware of the scribes, who like to walk in long robes, and to get greetings in the marketplaces,
WEB.39: and the best seats in the synagogues, and the best places at feasts:
WEB.40: those who devour widows’ houses, and for a pretense make long prayers. These will receive greater condemnation.”
WEB.41: Jesus sat down opposite the treasury, and saw how the multitude cast money into the treasury. Many who were rich cast in much.
WEB.42: A poor widow came, and she cast in two small brass coins,  which equal a quadrans coin.
WEB.43: He called his disciples to himself, and said to them, “Most certainly I tell you, this poor widow gave more than all those who are giving into the treasury,
WEB.44: for they all gave out of their abundance, but she, out of her poverty, gave all that she had to live on.”

AMP.1: Jesus began to speak to them [the chief priests, scribes and elders who were questioning Him] in parables: "A man planted a vineyard and put awall around it, and dug a pit for the wine press and built a tower; and he rented it out to tenant farmers and left the country.
AMP.2: When the harvest season came he sent a servant to the tenants, in order to collect from them some of the fruit of the vineyard.
AMP.3: They took him and beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
AMP.4: Again he sent them another servant, and they [threw stones and] wounded him in the head, and treated him disgracefully.
AMP.5: And he sent another, and that one they killed; then many others—some they beat and some they killed.
AMP.6: He still had one man left to send, a beloved son; he sent him last of all to them, saying, 'They will respect my son.'
AMP.7: But those tenants said to each other, 'This man is the heir! Come on, let us kill him [and destroy the evidence], and his inheritance will be ours!'
AMP.8: So they took him and killed him, and threw his body outside the vineyard.
AMP.9: What will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy the tenants, and will give the vineyard to others.
AMP.10:
Have you not even read this Scripture:
'The stone which the builders regarded as unworthy and rejected,
This [very stone] has become the chief Cornerstone
AMP.11:
This came about from the Lord,
And it is marvelous and wonderful in our eyes'?"
AMP.12: And they were looking for a way to seize Him, but they were afraid of the crowd; for they knew that He spoke this parable in reference to [and as a charge against] them. And so they left Him and went away.
AMP.13: Then they sent some of the Pharisees and Herodians to Jesus in order to trap Him into making a statement [that they could use against Him].
AMP.14: They came and said to Him, "Teacher, we know that You are truthful and have no personal bias toward anyone; for You are not influenced by outward appearances or social status, but in truth You teach the way of God. Is it lawful [according to Jewish law and tradition] to pay the poll-tax to [ Tiberius] Caesar, or not?
AMP.15: Should we pay [the tax] or should we not pay?" But knowing their hypocrisy, He asked them, "Why are you testing Me? Bring Me a coin (denarius) to look at."
AMP.16: So they brought one. Then He asked them, "Whose image and inscription is this?" They said to Him, "Caesar's."
AMP.17: Jesus said to them, "Pay to Caesar the things that are Caesar's, and to God the things that are God's." And they were greatly amazed at Him.
AMP.18: Some Sadducees (who say that there is no resurrection) came to Him, and began questioning Him, saying,
AMP.19: "Teacher, Moses wrote for us [a law] that if a man's brother dies and leaves a wife but leaves no child, his brother is tomarry the widow and raise up children for his brother.
AMP.20: There were seven brothers; the first [one] took a wife, and died leaving no children.
AMP.21: The second brother married her, and died leaving no children; and the third likewise;
AMP.22: and so all seven [married her and died, and] left no children. Last of all the woman died also.
AMP.23: In the resurrection, whose wife will she be? For all seven [brothers] were married to her."
AMP.24: Jesus said to them, "Is this not why you are wrong, because you know neither the Scriptures [that teach the resurrection] nor the power of God [who is able to raise the dead]?
AMP.25: For when they rise from the dead, they do not marry nor are they given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
AMP.26: But concerning the raising of the dead, have you not read in the book of Moses, in the passage about the burning bush, how God spoke to him, saying, 'I am the God of Abraham, and the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob'?
AMP.27: He is not the God of the dead, but of the living; you are greatly mistaken and you are deceiving yourselves!"
AMP.28: Then one of the scribes [an expert in Mosaic Law] came up and listened to them arguing [with one another], and noticing that Jesus answered them well, asked Him, "Which commandment is first and most important of all?"
AMP.29: Jesus answered, "The first and most important one is: 'Hear, O Israel, the Lord our God is one Lord;
AMP.30: <e>and you shall love the Lord your God with all your heart, and with all your soul</e> (life), <e>and with all your mind</e> (thought, understanding), <e>and with all your strength</e>.'
AMP.31: This is the second: 'You shall [unselfishly]love your neighbor as yourself.' There is no other commandment greater than these."
AMP.32: The scribe said to Him, "Admirably answered, Teacher; You truthfully stated that <e>He is One, and there is no other but Him</e>;
AMP.33: and to love Him with all the heart and with all the understanding and with all the strength, and to [unselfishly] love one's neighbor as oneself, is much more than all burnt offerings and sacrifices."
AMP.34: When Jesus saw that he answered thoughtfully and intelligently, He said to him, "You are not far from the kingdom of God." And after that, no one would dare to ask Him any more questions.
AMP.35: Jesus began to say, as He taught in [a portico or court of] the temple, " How can the scribes say that the Christ is the son of David?
AMP.36:
David himself said [when inspired] by the Holy Spirit,
'The Lord (the Father) said to my Lord (the Son, the Messiah),
"Sit at My right hand,
Until I put Your enemies under Your feet."'
AMP.37: David himself calls Him (the Son, the Messiah) 'Lord'; so how can it be that He is David's Son?" The large crowd enjoyed hearing Jesus and listened to Him with delight.
AMP.38: In [the course of] His teaching He was saying, "Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes [displaying their prominence], and like to receive respectful greetings in the market places,
AMP.39: and [they love] the chief seats in the synagogues and the places of distinction and honor at banquets,
AMP.40: [these scribes] who devour (confiscate) widows' houses, and offer long prayers for appearance's sake [to impress others]. These men will receive greater condemnation."
AMP.41: And He sat down opposite the [temple] treasury, and began watching how the people were putting money into the treasury. And many rich people were putting in large sums.
AMP.42: A poor widow came and put in two small copper coins, which amount to a mite.
AMP.43: Calling His disciples to Him, He said to them, "I assure you and most solemnly say to you, this poor widow put in [proportionally] more than all the contributors to the treasury.
AMP.44: For they all contributed from their surplus, but she, from her poverty, put in all she had, all she had to live on."

MSG.1: Then Jesus started telling them stories. "A man planted a vineyard. He fenced it, dug a winepress, erected a watchtower, turned it over to the farmhands, and went off on a trip.
MSG.2: At the time for harvest, he sent a servant back to the farmhands to collect his profits.
MSG.3: "They grabbed him, beat him up, and sent him off empty-handed.
MSG.4: So he sent another servant. That one they tarred and feathered.
MSG.5: He sent another and that one they killed. And on and on, many others. Some they beat up, some they killed.
MSG.6: "Finally there was only one left: a beloved son. In a last-ditch effort, he sent him, thinking, 'Surely they will respect my son.'
MSG.7: "But those farmhands saw their chance. They rubbed their hands together in greed and said, 'This is the heir! Let's kill him and have it all for ourselves.'
MSG.8: They grabbed him, killed him, and threw him over the fence.
MSG.9: "What do you think the owner of the vineyard will do? Right. He'll come and clean house. Then he'll assign the care of the vineyard to others.
MSG.10: Read it for yourselves in Scripture: That stone the masons threw out is now the cornerstone!
MSG.11: This is God's work; we rub our eyes—we can hardly believe it!"
MSG.12: They wanted to lynch him then and there but, intimidated by public opinion, held back. They knew the story was about them. They got away from there as fast as they could.
MSG.13: They sent some Pharisees and followers of Herod to bait him, hoping to catch him saying something incriminating.
MSG.14: They came up and said, "Teacher, we know you have integrity, that you are indifferent to public opinion, don't pander to your students, and teach the way of God accurately. Tell us: Is it lawful to pay taxes to Caesar or not?"
MSG.15: He knew it was a trick question, and said, "Why are you playing these games with me? Bring me a coin and let me look at it."
MSG.16: They handed him one. "This engraving—who does it look like? And whose name is on it?" "Caesar," they said.
MSG.17: Jesus said, "Give Caesar what is his, and give God what is his." Their mouths hung open, speechless.
MSG.18: Some Sadducees, the party that denies any possibility of resurrection, came up and asked,
MSG.19: "Teacher, Moses wrote that if a man dies and leaves a wife but no child, his brother is obligated to marry the widow and have children.
MSG.20: Well, there once were seven brothers. The first took a wife. He died childless.
MSG.21: The second married her. He died, and still no child. The same with the third.
MSG.22: All seven took their turn, but no child. Finally the wife died.
MSG.23: When they are raised at the resurrection, whose wife is she? All seven were her husband."
MSG.24: Jesus said, "You're way off base, and here's why: One, you don't know your Bibles; two, you don't know how God works.
MSG.25: After the dead are raised up, we're past the marriage business. As it is with angels now, all our ecstasies and intimacies then will be with God.
MSG.26: And regarding the dead, whether or not they are raised, don't you ever read the Bible? How God at the bush said to Moses, 'I am—not was—the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob'?
MSG.27: The living God is God of the living, not the dead. You're way, way off base."
MSG.28: One of the religion scholars came up. Hearing the lively exchanges of question and answer and seeing how sharp Jesus was in his answers, he put in his question: "Which is most important of all the commandments?"
MSG.29: Jesus said, "The first in importance is, 'Listen, Israel: The Lord your God is one;
MSG.30: so love the Lord God with all your passion and prayer and intelligence and energy.'
MSG.31: And here is the second: 'Love others as well as you love yourself.' There is no other commandment that ranks with these."
MSG.32: The religion scholar said, "A wonderful answer, Teacher! So lucid and accurate—that God is one and there is no other.
MSG.33: And loving him with all passion and intelligence and energy, and loving others as well as you love yourself. Why, that's better than all offerings and sacrifices put together!"
MSG.34: When Jesus realized how insightful he was, he said, "You're almost there, right on the border of God's kingdom." After that, no one else dared ask a question.
MSG.35: While he was teaching in the Temple, Jesus asked, "How is it that the religion scholars say that the Messiah is David's 'son,'
MSG.36: when we all know that David, inspired by the Holy Spirit, said, God said to my Master, "Sit here at my right hand until I put your enemies under your feet."
MSG.37: "David here designates the Messiah 'my Master' — so how can the Messiah also be his 'son'?" The large crowd was delighted with what they heard.
MSG.38: He continued teaching. "Watch out for the religion scholars. They love to walk around in academic gowns, preening in the radiance of public flattery,
MSG.39: basking in prominent positions, sitting at the head table at every church function.
MSG.40: And all the time they are exploiting the weak and helpless. The longer their prayers, the worse they get. But they'll pay for it in the end."
MSG.41: Sitting across from the offering box, he was observing how the crowd tossed money in for the collection. Many of the rich were making large contributions.
MSG.42: One poor widow came up and put in two small coins—a measly two cents.
MSG.43: Jesus called his disciples over and said, "The truth is that this poor widow gave more to the collection than all the others put together.
MSG.44: All the others gave what they'll never miss; she gave extravagantly what she couldn't afford—she gave her all."

NIV.1:
The Parable of the Tenants
He then began to speak to them in parables: “A man planted a vineyard. He put a wall around it, dug a pit for the winepress and built a watchtower. Then he rented the vineyard to some farmers and went away on a journey.
NIV.2: At harvest time he sent a servant to the tenants to collect from them some of the fruit of the vineyard.
NIV.3: But they seized him, beat him and sent him away empty-handed.
NIV.4: Then he sent another servant to them; they struck this man on the head and treated him shamefully.
NIV.5: He sent still another, and that one they killed. He sent many others; some of them they beat, others they killed.
NIV.6: “He had one left to send, a son, whom he loved. He sent him last of all, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
NIV.7: “But the tenants said to one another, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him, and the inheritance will be ours.’
NIV.8: So they took him and killed him, and threw him out of the vineyard.
NIV.9: “What then will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and kill those tenants and give the vineyard to others.
NIV.10:
Haven’t you read this scripture:
“‘The stone the builders rejected
has become the capstone;
NIV.11:
the Lord has done this,
and it is marvelous in our eyes’?”
NIV.12: Then they looked for a way to arrest him because they knew he had spoken the parable against them. But they were afraid of the crowd; so they left him and went away.
NIV.13:
Paying Taxes to Caesar
Later they sent some of the Pharisees and Herodians to Jesus to catch him in his words.
NIV.14: They came to him and said, “Teacher, we know you are a man of integrity. You aren’t swayed by men, because you pay no attention to who they are; but you teach the way of God in accordance with the truth. Is it right to pay taxes to Caesar or not?
NIV.15:
Should we pay or shouldn’t we?”
But Jesus knew their hypocrisy. “Why are you trying to trap me?” he asked. “Bring me a denarius and let me look at it.”
NIV.16:
They brought the coin, and he asked them, “Whose portrait is this? And whose inscription?”
“Caesar’s,” they replied.
NIV.17:
Then Jesus said to them, “Give to Caesar what is Caesar’s and to God what is God’s.”
And they were amazed at him.
NIV.18:
Marriage at the Resurrection
Then the Sadducees, who say there is no resurrection, came to him with a question.
NIV.19: “Teacher,” they said, “Moses wrote for us that if a man’s brother dies and leaves a wife but no children, the man must marry the widow and have children for his brother.
NIV.20: Now there were seven brothers. The first one married and died without leaving any children.
NIV.21: The second one married the widow, but he also died, leaving no child. It was the same with the third.
NIV.22: In fact, none of the seven left any children. Last of all, the woman died too.
NIV.23: At the resurrection whose wife will she be, since the seven were married to her?”
NIV.24: Jesus replied, “Are you not in error because you do not know the Scriptures or the power of God?
NIV.25: When the dead rise, they will neither marry nor be given in marriage; they will be like the angels in heaven.
NIV.26: Now about the dead rising—have you not read in the book of Moses, in the account of the bush, how God said to him, ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’?
NIV.27: He is not the God of the dead, but of the living. You are badly mistaken!”
NIV.28:
The Greatest Commandment
One of the teachers of the law came and heard them debating. Noticing that Jesus had given them a good answer, he asked him, “Of all the commandments, which is the most important?”
NIV.29: “The most important one,” answered Jesus, “is this: ‘Hear, O Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is one.
NIV.30: Love the Lord your God with all your heart and with all your soul and with all your mind and with all your strength.’
NIV.31: The second is this: ‘Love your neighbor as yourself.’ There is no commandment greater than these.”
NIV.32: “Well said, teacher,” the man replied. “You are right in saying that God is one and there is no other but him.
NIV.33: To love him with all your heart, with all your understanding and with all your strength, and to love your neighbor as yourself is more important than all burnt offerings and sacrifices.”
NIV.34: When Jesus saw that he had answered wisely, he said to him, “You are not far from the kingdom of God.” And from then on no one dared ask him any more questions.
NIV.35:
Whose Son Is the Christ?
While Jesus was teaching in the temple courts, he asked, “How is it that the teachers of the law say that the Christ is the son of David?
NIV.36:
David himself, speaking by the Holy Spirit, declared:
“‘The Lord said to my Lord:
“Sit at my right hand
until I put your enemies
under your feet.”’
NIV.37:
David himself calls him ‘Lord.’ How then can he be his son?”
The large crowd listened to him with delight.
NIV.38: As he taught, Jesus said, “Watch out for the teachers of the law. They like to walk around in flowing robes and be greeted in the marketplaces,
NIV.39: and have the most important seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at banquets.
NIV.40: They devour widows’ houses and for a show make lengthy prayers. Such men will be punished most severely.”
NIV.41:
The Widow’s Offering
Jesus sat down opposite the place where the offerings were put and watched the crowd putting their money into the temple treasury. Many rich people threw in large amounts.
NIV.42: But a poor widow came and put in two very small copper coins, worth only a fraction of a penny.
NIV.43: Calling his disciples to him, Jesus said, “I tell you the truth, this poor widow has put more into the treasury than all the others.
NIV.44: They all gave out of their wealth; but she, out of her poverty, put in everything—all she had to live on.”

NRSVCE.33: and ‘to love him with all the heart, and with all the understanding, and with all the strength,’ and ‘to love one’s neighbor as oneself,’ — this is much more important than all whole burnt offerings and sacrifices.”
NRSVCE.34: When Jesus saw that he answered wisely, he said to him, “You are not far from the kingdom of God.” After that no one dared to ask him any question.
NRSVCE.35: While Jesus was teaching in the temple, he said, “How can the scribes say that the Messiah is the son of David?
NRSVCE.36:
David himself, by the Holy Spirit, declared, ‘The Lord said to my Lord,
“Sit at my right hand,
until I put your enemies under your feet.”’
NRSVCE.37: David himself calls him Lord; so how can he be his son?” And the large crowd was listening to him with delight.
NRSVCE.38: As he taught, he said, “Beware of the scribes, who like to walk around in long robes, and to be greeted with respect in the marketplaces,
NRSVCE.39: and to have the best seats in the synagogues and places of honor at banquets!
NRSVCE.40: They devour widows’ houses and for the sake of appearance say long prayers. They will receive the greater condemnation.”
NRSVCE.41: He sat down opposite the treasury, and watched the crowd putting money into the treasury. Many rich people put in large sums.
NRSVCE.42: A poor widow came and put in two small copper coins, which are worth a penny.
NRSVCE.43: Then he called his disciples and said to them, “Truly I tell you, this poor widow has put in more than all those who are contributing to the treasury.
NRSVCE.44: For all of them have contributed out of their abundance; but she out of her poverty has put in everything she had, all she had to live on.”

NET.1: Then he began to speak to them in parables: “A man planted a vineyard. He put a fence around it, dug a pit for its winepress, and built a watchtower. Then he leased it to tenant farmers and went on a journey.
NET.2: At harvest time he sent a slave to the tenants to collect from them his portion of the crop.
NET.3: But those tenants seized his slave, beat him, and sent him away empty-handed.
NET.4: So he sent another slave to them again. This one they struck on the head and treated outrageously.
NET.5: He sent another, and that one they killed. This happened to many others, some of whom were beaten, others killed.
NET.6: He had one left, his one dear son. Finally he sent him to them, saying, ‘They will respect my son.’
NET.7: But those tenants said to one another, ‘This is the heir. Come, let’s kill him and the inheritance will be ours!’
NET.8: So they seized him, killed him, and threw his body out of the vineyard.
NET.9: What then will the owner of the vineyard do? He will come and destroy those tenants and give the vineyard to others.
NET.10: Have you not read this scripture: ‘The stone the builders rejected has become the cornerstone.
NET.11: This is from the Lord, and it is marvelous in our eyes’?”
NET.12: Now they wanted to arrest him (but they feared the crowd), because they realized that he told this parable against them. So they left him and went away.
NET.13: Then they sent some of the Pharisees and Herodians to trap him with his own words.
NET.14: When they came they said to him, “Teacher, we know that you are truthful and do not court anyone’s favor, because you show no partiality but teach the way of God in accordance with the truth. Is it right to pay taxes to Caesar or not? Should we pay or shouldn’t we?”
NET.15: But he saw through their hypocrisy and said to them, “Why are you testing me? Bring me a denarius and let me look at it.”
NET.16: So they brought one, and he said to them, “Whose image is this, and whose inscription?” They replied, “Caesar’s.”
NET.17: Then Jesus said to them, “Give to Caesar the things that are Caesar’s, and to God the things that are God’s.” And they were utterly amazed at him.
NET.18: Sadducees (who say there is no resurrection) also came to him and asked him,
NET.19: “Teacher, Moses wrote for us: ‘If a man’s brother dies and leaves a wife but no children, that man must marry the widow and father children for his brother.’
NET.20: There were seven brothers. The first one married, and when he died he had no children.
NET.21: The second married her and died without any children, and likewise the third.
NET.22: None of the seven had children. Finally, the woman died too.
NET.23: In the resurrection, when they rise again, whose wife will she be? For all seven had married her.”
NET.24: Jesus said to them, “Aren’t you deceived for this reason, because you don’t know the scriptures or the power of God?
NET.25: For when they rise from the dead, they neither marry nor are given in marriage, but are like angels in heaven.
NET.26: Now as for the dead being raised, have you not read in the book of Moses, in the passage about the bush, how God said to him, ‘I am the God of Abraham, the God of Isaac, and the God of Jacob’?
NET.27: He is not the God of the dead but of the living. You are badly mistaken!”
NET.28: Now one of the experts in the law came and heard them debating. When he saw that Jesus answered them well, he asked him, “Which commandment is the most important of all?”
NET.29: Jesus answered, “The most important is: ‘Listen, Israel, the Lord our God, the Lord is one.
NET.30: Love the Lord your God with all your heart, with all your soul, with all your mind, and with all your strength.’
NET.31: The second is: ‘Love your neighbor as yourself.’ There is no other commandment greater than these.”
NET.32: The expert in the law said to him, “That is true, Teacher; you are right to say that he is one, and there is no one else besides him.
NET.33: And to love him with all your heart, with all your mind, and with all your strength and to love your neighbor as yourself is more important than all burnt offerings and sacrifices.”
NET.34: When Jesus saw that he had answered thoughtfully, he said to him, “You are not far from the kingdom of God.” Then no one dared any longer to question him.
NET.35: While Jesus was teaching in the temple courts, he said, “How is it that the experts in the law say that the Christ is David’s son?
NET.36: David himself, by the Holy Spirit, said, ‘The Lord said to my lord, “Sit at my right hand, until I put your enemies under your feet.”’
NET.37: If David himself calls him ‘Lord,’ how can he be his son?” And the large crowd was listening to him with delight.
NET.38: In his teaching Jesus also said, “Watch out for the experts in the law. They like walking around in long robes and elaborate greetings in the marketplaces,
NET.39: and the best seats in the synagogues and the places of honor at banquets.
NET.40: They devour widows’ property, and as a show make long prayers. These men will receive a more severe punishment.”
NET.41: Then he sat down opposite the offering box, and watched the crowd putting coins into it. Many rich people were throwing in large amounts.
NET.42: And a poor widow came and put in two small copper coins, worth less than a penny.
NET.43: He called his disciples and said to them, “I tell you the truth, this poor widow has put more into the offering box than all the others.
NET.44: For they all gave out of their wealth. But she, out of her poverty, put in what she had to live on, everything she had.”
